title: Chiropractic Medicare Coverage Modernization Act of 2025
short_title: Chiropractic Medicare Coverage Modernization Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill changes Medicare to cover more chiropractic services than it does now. Currently, Medicare only pays for chiropractors to adjust the spine, but this bill would let Medicare pay for any service that chiropractors are allowed to do in their state. Chiropractors would need to take a one-time online training class to provide these new services, but they can keep doing spine adjustments without taking the class. This gives older Americans on Medicare access to more types of chiropractic care.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Chiropractic Medicare Coverage Modernization Act of two thousand twenty-five.

sections.title: Section 2  Findings; Statement of Purpose
sections.text: Congress has found several important facts. In nineteen seventy-two, Medicare started covering chiropractic care for people who receive Medicare benefits. However, the old law limits beneficiaries to only one type of service at a chiropractic clinic. Medicare coverage for chiropractic care has not kept up with what private insurance companies cover or what other federal health programs offer. Today, research shows that chiropractic services work well and cost less money. Because of this good evidence, private insurance coverage for chiropractic services has grown. State licenses for chiropractors have also improved to better serve patients and improve health results. This law would make Medicare chiropractic coverage more similar to what is provided by the Department of Veterans Affairs, the Department of Defense, the Federal Employee Health Benefits Program, and private health insurance. The purpose of this law is to expand how Medicare recognizes and covers doctors of chiropractic as physicians under the Medicare program. This includes their current service of manual manipulation of the spine to correct a subluxation (a misalignment of the spine) and any other function or action that the state legally allows them to perform.

sections.title: Section 3  Providing Medicare Coverage for All Physicians' Services Furnished by Doctors of Chiropractic Within the Scope of Their License
sections.text: This section changes the Social Security Act to expand what chiropractic services Medicare will cover. Currently, Medicare only covers chiropractors for manual manipulation of the spine to correct a subluxation if they are licensed by their state and meet minimum standards set by the Secretary of Health and Human Services. The new law changes this to cover any doctor of chiropractic who is licensed as a doctor of chiropractic or chiropractor by their state. Medicare will now cover any function or action that their state license legally allows them to perform. However, there are some limits on payment for these expanded services. Medicare will only pay for these new expanded services if the doctor of chiropractic has completed an educational webinar designed by the Secretary of Health and Human Services or an updated version of this webinar. The doctor only needs to complete this verification process once. Medicare will continue to pay for the traditional spine manipulation services without requiring this webinar.
